subject: Phase 2B SHIPPED — images published + public (v0.4.9 / wrapper 0.2.11); app-side gate can flip to live
body:
Phase 2B (wrapper endpoint + Pod-image rebuild) is DONE.

WRAPPER: POST /wrapper/models/upload added (mpi-ci commit 249ea37). _land_on_models = twin of _land_on_volume, validates type+basename via existing _model_dest, lands at MODELS_DIR/<type>/<basename>, creates the model subdir on a fresh volume. Multipart contract matches the app exactly: file, filename (bare basename), type ('loras'|'upscale_models'), overwrite ('true'). Framework-free test green (test_models_upload.py). /wrapper/models/status + _is_complete_on_disk UNTOUCHED (already answer the presence check). Wrapper 0.2.10 -> 0.2.11.

IMAGES: Pod image tag v0.4.9. THREE public tags live + anon-pullable:
  ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.4.9-cu124
  ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.4.9-cu128
  ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.4.9-cpu
cu124+cpu via mpi-ci CI; cu128 built local + pushed. ComfyUI layer was CACHE-REUSED from v0.4.8, so NO ComfyUI master drift despite comfyui_ref=master. torch 2.7.1+cu128 verified on cu128.

APP-SIDE GATE (flip to live, app-only edit — NOT done by this agent): in routes/remoteProxy.js bump POD_IMAGE_VERSION 'v0.4.8' -> 'v0.4.9' AND WRAPPER_VERSION '0.2.10' -> '0.2.11', then RESTART THE APP (the Express child bakes both at boot; a live app keeps sending v0.4.8 until restart). Verify: app-log image line shows v0.4.9 + GET /health wrapper_version=0.2.11. Until restart the upload POST 404s against the old image (app handles 404 as a clean failure toast, not a crash).

THEN Phase 3 live-verify (USER, live Pod): (1) remote gen with a Pod-absent local LoRA -> 'Uploading...' toast -> file lands /workspace/mpi_models/loras/ -> gen uses it. (2) repro that opened this card: custom upscaler 1xDeNoise_realplksr_otf.pth -> now uploads + produces output. (3) re-gen same model -> presence true -> NO re-upload. (4) Pod reset (volume wiped) -> next gen re-uploads.

Housekeeping: 2 junk tags vv0.4.9-cu124 / vv0.4.9-cpu exist on GHCR (double-v from a first bad dispatch; app never requests them, harmless). Delete via GHCR web UI when convenient — CLI token lacks delete:packages scope.
